verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He took His garments, and having sat down again, said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A grievous thing, beloved, a grievous thing it is to come to the depths of wickedness; for then the soul becomes hard to be restored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore we should use every exertion not to be taken at all; since it is easier not to fall in, than having fallen to recover one’s self.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, for instance, when Judas had thrown himself into sin, how great assistance he enjoyed, yet not even so was he raised.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ said to him, “One of you is a devil” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 71 ); He said, “Not all believe” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 65 ); He said, “I speak not of all,” and, “I know whom I have chosen” ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ); and not one of these sayings doth he feel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now when He had washed their feet, and taken His garments, and sat down, He said, “Know ye what I have done unto you?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He no longer addresseth Himself to Peter only, but to them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye call Me Lord and Master, and ye say well, for so I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye call Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He taketh to Him their judgment, and then that the words may not be thought to be words of their kindness, He addeth, “for so I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By introducing a saying of theirs, He maketh it not offensive, and by confirming it Himself when introduced from them, unsuspected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For so I am,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how when He converseth with the disciples, He speaketh revealing more what belongeth unto Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As He saith, “Call no man master on earth, for One is your guide” ( Matt. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8, 9 ), so also, “And call no man father upon earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the “one” and “one” is spoken not of the Father only, but of Himself also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He spoken excluding Himself, how saith He, “That ye may become the children of the light”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, if He called the Father only, “Master,” how saith He, “For so I am”; and again, “For one is your Guide, even Christ”? ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I then,” He saith, “your Lord and Master have washed your feet, ye ought also to wash one another’s feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet it is not the same thing, for He is Lord and Master, but ye are fellow-servants one of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth then the “as”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “With the same zeal.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For on this account He taketh instances from greater actions that we may, if so be, perform the less.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus schoolmasters write the letters for children very beautifully, that they may come to imitate them though but in an inferior manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Where now are they who spit on their fellow-servants? where now they who demand honors?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ washed the feet of the traitor, the sacrilegious, the thief, and that close to the time of the betrayal, and incurable as he was, made him a partaker of His table; and art thou highminded, and dost thou draw up thine eyebrows?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let us then wash one another’s feet,” saith some one, “then we must wash those of our domestics.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what great thing if we do wash even those of our domestics?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In our case “slave” and “free” is a difference of words; but there an actual reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by nature He was Lord and we servants, yet even this He refused not at this time to do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now it is matter for contentment if we do not treat free men as bondmen, as slaves bought with money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what shall we say in that day, if after receiving proofs of such forbearance, we ourselves do not imitate them at all, but take the contrary part, being in diametrical opposition, lifted up, and not discharging the debt?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God hath made us debtors one to another, having first so done Himself, and hath made us debtors of a less amount.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He was our Lord, but we do it, if we do it at all, to our fellow-servants, a thing which He Himself implied by saying, “If I then your Lord and Master—so also do ye.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It would indeed naturally have followed to say, “How much more should ye servants,” but He left this to the conscience of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why hath He done this “now”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They were for the future to enjoy, some greater, some less honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order then that they may not exalt themselves one above the other, and say as they did before, “Who is the greatest” ( Matt. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ), nor be angry one against the other, He taketh down the high thoughts of them all, by saying, that “although thou mayest be very great, thou oughtest to have no high thoughts towards thy brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He mentioned not the greater action, that “if I have washed the feet of the traitor, what great matter if ye one another’s?” but having exemplified this by deeds, He then left it to the judgment of the spectators.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He said, “Whosoever shall do and teach, the same shall be called great” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ); for this is “to teach” a thing, actually to do it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What pride should not this remove? what kind of folly and insolence should it not annihilate!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who sitteth upon the Cherubim washed the feet of the traitor, and dost thou, O man, thou that art earth and ashes and cinders and dust, dost thou exalt thyself, and art thou highminded?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how great a hell wouldest thou not deserve?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then thou desirest a high state of mind, come, I will show thee the way to it; for thou dost not even know what it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The man then who gives heed to the present things as being great, is of a mean soul; so that there can neither be humility without greatness of soul, nor conceit except from littleness of soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as little children are eager for trifles, gaping upon balls and hoops and dice, but cannot even form an idea of important matters; so in this case, one who is truly wise, will deem present things as nothing, (so that he will neither choose to acquire them himself, nor to receive them from others;) but he who is not of such a character will be affected in a contrary way, intent upon cobwebs and shadows and dreams of things less substantial than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you, the servant is not greater than his lord, neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I speak not of you all —but that the Scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with Me hath lifted up his heel against Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He said before, this He saith here also, to shame them; “For if the servant is not greater than his master, nor he that is sent greater than him that sent him, and these things have been done by Me, much more ought they to be done by you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, lest any one should say, “Why now sayest Thou these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do we not already know them?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He addeth this very thing, “I speak not to you as not knowing, but that by your actions ye may show forth the things spoken of.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “to know,” belongeth to all; but “to do,” not to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He said, “Blessed are ye if ye do them”; and on this account I continually and ever say the same to you, although ye know it, that I may set you on the work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since even Jews “know,” but yet they are not “blessed”; for they do not what they know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I speak not,” He saith, “of you all.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O what forbearance!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not yet doth He convict the traitor, but veileth the matter, hence giving him room for repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He convicteth and yet doth not convict him when He saith thus, “He that eateth bread with Me hath lifted up his heel against Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It seems to me that the, “The servant is not greater than his lord,” was uttered for this purpose also, that if any persons should at any time suffer harm either from domestics or from any of the meaner sort, they should not be offended; looking to the instance of Judas, who having enjoyed ten thousand good things, repaid his Benefactor with the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He added, “He that eateth bread with Me,” and letting pass all the rest, He hath put that which was most fitted to restrain and shame him; “he who was fed by Me,” He saith, “and who shared My table.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He spake the words, to instruct them to benefit those who did evil to them, even though such persons should continue incurable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But having said, “I speak not of you all,” in order not to attach fear to more than one, He at last separateth the traitor, speaking thus; “He that eateth bread with Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the, “not of you all,” doth not direct the words to any single one, therefore He added, “He that eateth bread with Me”; showing to that wretched one that He was not seized in ignorance, but even with full knowledge; a thing which of itself was most of all fitted to restrain him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He said not, “betrayeth Me,” but, “hath lifted up his heel against Me,” desiring to represent the deceit, the treachery, the secrecy of the plot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things are written that we bear not malice towards those who injure us; but rebuke them and weep for them; for the fit subjects of weeping are not they who suffer, but they who do the wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The grasping man, the false accuser, and whoso worketh any other evil thing, do themselves the greatest injury, and us the greatest good, if we do not avenge ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a case as this: some one has robbed thee; hast thou given thanks for the injury, and glorified God? by that thanksgiving thou hast gained ten thousand rewards, just as he hath gathered for himself fire unspeakable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if any one say, “How then, if I ‘could’ not defend myself against him who wronged me, being weaker?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I would say this, that thou couldest have put into action the being discontented, the being impatient, (for these things are in our power,) the praying against him, who grieved you, the uttering ten thousand curses against him, the speaking ill of him to every one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore who hath not done these things shall even be rewarded for not defending himself, since it is clear that even if he had had the power, he would not have done it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The injured man uses any weapon that comes to hand, when, being little of soul, he defends himself against one who has injured him, by curses, by abuse, by plotting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do thou then not only not do these things, but even pray for him; for if thou do them not, but wilt even pray for him, thou art become like unto God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “pray,” it saith, “for them, that despitefully use you—that ye may be like unto your Father which is in Heaven.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how we are the greatest gainers from the insolence of others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing so delighteth God, as the not returning evil for evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what say I?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not returning evil for evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely we are enjoined to return the opposite, benefits, prayers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ also repaid him who was about to betray Him with everything opposite.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He washed his feet, convicted him secretly, rebuked him sparingly, tended him, allowed him to share His table and His kiss, and not even by these was he made better; nevertheless (Christ) continued doing His own part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But come, let us teach thee even from the example of servants, and (to make the lesson stronger) those in the Old (Testament), that thou mayest know that we have no ground of defense when we remember a wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will you then that I tell you of Moses, or shall we go yet farther back?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the more ancient the instances that can be pointed out, the more are we surpassed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because virtue was then more difficult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those men had no written precepts, no patterns of living, but their nature fought, unarmed, by itself, and was forced to float in all directions unballasted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also when praising Noah, God called him not simply perfect, but added, “in his generation” ( Gen. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ); signifying, “at that time,” when there were many hindrances, since many others shone after him, yet will he have nothing less than they; for in his own time he was perfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who then before Moses was patient?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The blessed and noble Joseph, who having shone by his chastity, shone no less by his long suffering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was sold when he had done no wrong, but was waiting on others, and serving, and performing all the duties of domestics.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They brought against him an evil accusation, and he did not defend himself, though he had his father on his side.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, he even went to carry food to them in the desert, and when he found them not, he did not despair or turn back, (yet he had an excuse for doing so had he chosen,) but remained near the wild beasts and those savage men, preserving the feeling of a true brother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, when he dwelt in the prison house, and was asked the cause, he spake no evil of them, but only, “I have done nothing,” and, “I was stolen out of the land of the Hebrews”; and after this again, when he was made lord, he nourished them, and delivered them from ten thousand dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we be sober, the wickedness of our neighbor is not strong enough to cast us out of our own virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But those others were not like him; they both stripped him, and endeavored to kill him, and reproach him with his dream, though they had even received their meat from him, and planned to deprive him of life and of liberty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they ate, and cared not for their brother lying naked in the pit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What could be worse than such brutality?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Were they not worse than any number of murderers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And after this, having drawn him up, they gave him over to ten thousand deaths, selling him to barbarian and savage men, who were on their journey to barbarians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet he, when he became ruler, not only remitted them their punishment, but even acquitted them, as far at least as relating to himself, of their sin, calling what had been done a dispensation of God, not any wickedness of theirs; and the things which he did against them he did not as remembering evil, but in all these he dissembled, for his brother’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After this, when he saw them clinging to him, he straightway threw away the mask, and wept aloud, and embraced them, as though he had received the greatest benefits, he, who formerly was made away with by them, and he brought them all down into Egypt, and repaid them with ten thousand benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What excuse then shall we have, if after the Law, and after grace, and after the addition of so much heavenly wisdom, we do not even strive to rival him who lived before grace and before the Law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who shall deliver us from punishment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there is nothing, there is nothing more grievous than the remembrance of injuries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this the man hath showed that owed ten thousand talents; from whom payment was at one time not demanded, at another time again demanded; not demanded, because of the lovingkindness of God; but demanded, because of his own wickedness, and because of his malice toward his fellow-servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing all which things, let us forgive our neighbors their trespasses, and repay them by deeds of an opposite kind, that we too may obtain mercy from God, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and dominion for ever and ever.
